source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Ansible Galaxy: Ansible roles and installing a community role

options: A unit that promotes the reusability of playbooks
questions.why: A role is a reusable unit bundling tasks, variables, templates, and so on.

questions.q: A key benefit of roles is that they...
options: Remove the need to manually include files or specify file paths in playbooks
questions.why: With a role you just specify a task; Ansible handles the inclusions, so no include directives or file paths.

questions.q: Which directive includes a role from within a task list?
questions.why: include_role includes a role as a task, naming the role.

questions.q: Where are installed roles stored by default (per the example output)?
questions.why: Installed roles live under the user's ~/.ansible/roles/ (e.g. /home/ansible/.ansible/roles/).

questions.q: How is a role applied through a play's `roles:` key?
options: Listed as an entry, e.g. - role: rocky8 (with become/become_user as needed)
questions.why: The roles: section lists the roles to apply, each as - role: <name>.

questions.q: The patchmanagement role lets you inject your own pre/post-update steps via...
options: Variables pointing to custom task files (pm_before/after_update_tasks_file)
questions.why: You point variables at your custom_tasks/*.yml files; the role includes them before and after updating.
